projects.title: INTENT PARSING LAYER
mainContent: ArbiPilot begins with natural language intent. A user can describe what they want in plain terms, and the system transforms that into structured, reviewable execution intent instead of jumping directly to opaque transaction creation.
This matters because blockchain actions demand precision. The parsing layer exists to reduce ambiguity without pretending that flexible human language should directly control money-moving execution.

projects.title: VALIDATION & GUARDRAILS
mainContent: ArbiPilot does not let the model freely decide what contracts to touch. Supported actions move through validation rules, allowlists, schema checks, and execution boundaries before they become eligible for signing.
The point is not to make AI more powerful. The point is to make AI-assisted onchain execution safer, narrower, and easier to reason about.

projects.title: RISK PREVIEW SYSTEM
mainContent: Before users sign, ArbiPilot explains what the action will do, what assets are involved, and which constraints are applied. The goal is to replace blind confidence with visible execution context.
This is one of the core differences between ArbiPilot and typical AI wallet flows: users are shown an intelligible action path before the wallet prompt becomes the point of no return.

details.outputs: Execution summary ; route context ; risk surface

projects.title: DETERMINISTIC SWAP EXECUTION
mainContent: ArbiPilot currently uses swap execution as its first constrained onchain action. That makes it a practical demonstration of the broader thesis: AI may help interpret intent, but execution should stay deterministic and reviewable.
By starting with a narrow action set, the system can provide more confidence, clearer boundaries, and a stronger foundation for future expansion.

projects.title: AGENT IDENTITY REGISTRY
mainContent: ArbiPilot is designed to register through the agent identity registry instead of acting like an invisible script with signing power. Identity matters when an agent influences wallet execution.
That makes the system more legible not only for users, but also for judges, developers, and future integrations that need to know who or what is initiating execution.

projects.title: ARBIPILOT INTERFACE
mainContent: The interface is not just a pretty shell around a transaction button. It is the place where intent, validation, explanation, and execution are brought together into a clearer user flow.
ArbiPilot's product surface exists to make agentic finance feel less like blind signing and more like supervised execution.
